description_long: This is a classic single-propeller airplane with a streamlined fuselage and a high-mounted straight wing configuration. The body is primarily a muted maroon color with lighter gray wings and tail surfaces, and the cockpit is positioned at the front of the fuselage, enclosed with a clear canopy. The airplane features a conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers, and the landing gear is fixed in a taildragger arrangement, with two main wheels under the wings and a smaller tail wheel. The propeller is mounted at the nose, and the engine cowling is rounded and prominent. The surface finish appears smooth and slightly glossy, with visible panel lines and structural details. This type of airplane is typical of small general aviation or vintage aircraft, suitable for short flights and private use.
description: A vintage-style, single-propeller airplane with high-mounted straight wings, maroon and gray coloring, and a fixed taildragger landing gear.
description_view_0: Side view showing the high-mounted wing, enclosed cockpit, and prominent nose-mounted propeller.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the straight wingspan, propeller, and fixed landing gear beneath the wings.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the taildragger gear, single vertical stabilizer, and streamlined fuselage.
description_view_3: Direct nose-on view emphasizing the propeller, cockpit canopy, and symmetrical wing structure.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: High-wing, straight configuration with no visible winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single nose-mounted piston engine with a two-blade propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed taildragger landing gear with two main wheels and a single tail wheel.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed single-seat cockpit with a clear canopy.
